g.data-narration: Welcome to Distributed Hash Tables - the backbone of decentralized data storage systems.
g.text: Enabling Decentralized Data Storage in Blockchain Networks

g.data-narration: A DHT is a distributed system that maps keys to values across many network nodes, enabling efficient lookup.
g.text: What is a Distributed Hash Table?
A DHT is a decentralized data structure that distributes key-value pairs across
a network of nodes, enabling scalable and efficient data retrieval without central servers.

g.data-narration: DHTs solve critical problems in blockchain: decentralization, scalability, and fault tolerance for peer-to-peer networks.
DHT architecture consists of nodes with unique IDs, a key space, and routing mechanisms for data lookup.
Node IDs are generated using hash functions that map node identifiers to unique values in the key space.
g.text: Node ID Generation using Hash Functions

g.data-narration: The key space is a circular range where each node is responsible for a portion of keys based on proximity to their node ID.
g.text: This determines data ownership in the DHT.
Example: Node ID "a7f3" stores keys a4f0 to a7f3

g.data-narration: DHT routing uses progressive neighbor queries - each node forwards requests to closest nodes until the target is found.
DHTs store data across multiple nodes - no single point of failure, enabling true decentralized data ownership.
DHTs scale horizontally - adding more nodes increases storage and lookup capacity while maintaining O(log N) performance.
g.text: ✓ New nodes join automatically • No rebalancing required • Routing tables update dynamically

g.data-narration: DHTs achieve fault tolerance through replication - data is stored on multiple nodes for redundancy against failures.
Major DHT algorithms include Chord (consistent hashing), Kademlia (XOR routing), and Pastry ( Plaxton mesh).
g.text: Kademlia used by BitTorrent/IPFS: XOR distance provides symmetric, triangle-inequality properties

g.data-narration: DHTs power blockchain applications: decentralized file storage (IPFS), smart contract data, and P2P DApps.
BitTorrent uses DHT for peer discovery, while IPFS uses DHT for content routing in decentralized storage.
g.text: Uses Kademlia DHT for peer discovery • Trackers replaced by DHT
Enables trackerless torrents • Millions of simultaneous users
Content-addressed storage • DHT for content routing
Files identified by hash • Git-like versioning • Powers Filecoin

g.data-narration: DHT security challenges include Sybil attacks, Eclipse attacks, and data poisoning - requiring careful mitigation strategies.
g.text: Mitigations: Identity verification • Reputation systems • Encryption • Redundancy

g.data-narration: In summary, DHTs enable decentralized, scalable, and fault-tolerant data storage essential for blockchain ecosystems.
g.text: ✓ DHTs: Decentralized key-value storage across peer nodes
✓ Architecture: Node IDs, Key Space, Routing (O(log N) lookup)
✓ Applications: IPFS, BitTorrent, DApps, Smart Contract Storage
DHTs are fundamental infrastructure for decentralized systems!

g.data-narration: For exams, remember: DHT architecture, Kademlia/BitTorrent/IPFS, O(log N) routing, and fault tolerance mechanisms.
g.text: 1. Know DHT architecture (Node ID → Key Space → Routing)
